Test Case: Verify user can successfully register with valid data
Pre-Conditions: System is running and accessible
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/auth/register with valid user data
2. Verify response status code
3. Check response message
Post-Condition: User account created in database with emailVerified=false

Test Case: Verify user can verify email with valid OTP
Pre-Conditions: User registered but not verified, OTP code available
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/auth/verify-email with email and OTP
2. Verify response status code
3. Check JWT token is returned
Post-Condition: User emailVerified flag set to true in database

Test Case: Verify verified user can login successfully
Pre-Conditions: User registered and email verified
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/auth/login with valid credentials
2. Verify response status code
3. Check JWT token is returned
Post-Condition: User successfully authenticated

Test Case: Verify authenticated user can retrieve their profile
Pre-Conditions: User logged in with valid JWT token
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /api/users/profile with Authorization header
2. Verify response status code
3. Check profile data is returned
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 200
Response contains: {"id": 1, "name": "John Doe", "telephone": "+852 9123 4567", "email": "john.doe@test.com", "customerType": "PERSONAL", "emailVerified": true, "createdAt": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Profile data retrieved successfully

Test Case: Verify system returns list of all vehicles
Pre-Conditions: System has vehicles in database
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /api/vehicles
2. Verify response status code
3. Check vehicles array is returned
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 200
Response is an array containing vehicle objects with fields: id, modelNumber, description, pictureUrl, features, unitPrice
Post-Condition: Vehicle list retrieved successfully

Test Case: Verify system returns specific vehicle details
Pre-Conditions: Vehicle with ID=1 exists in database
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /api/vehicles/1
2. Verify response status code
3. Check vehicle details are returned
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 200
Response contains: {"id": 1, "modelNumber": "<model>", "description": "<desc>", "pictureUrl": "<url>", "features": "<features>", "unitPrice": <price>}
Post-Condition: Vehicle details retrieved successfully

Test Case: Verify system returns list of upcoming seminars
Pre-Conditions: System has future seminars in database
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /api/seminars/upcoming
2. Verify response status code
3. Check seminars array is returned with availability info
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 200
Response is an array containing seminar objects with fields: id, vehicleId, vehicleModelNumber, vehicleDescription, seminarDate, maxSeats, bookedSeats, availableSeats
Post-Condition: Seminar list retrieved successfully

Test Case: Verify authenticated user can register for a seminar
Pre-Conditions: User logged in, seminar with available seats exists
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register with Authorization header
2. Include seatsBooked in request body
3. Verify response status code
4. Check registration status
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 200
Response contains: {"id": <reg_id>, "seminarId": 1, "vehicleModelNumber": "<model>", "seminarDate": "<date>", "seatsBooked": 2, "status": "SUCCESS", "createdAt": "<timestamp>", "updatedAt": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Registration created with SUCCESS status, seats deducted from available

Test Case: Verify authenticated user can view their registration history
Pre-Conditions: User logged in and has at least one registration
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /api/registrations/history with Authorization header
2. Verify response status code
3. Check registrations array is returned
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 200
Response is an array containing registration objects with fields: id, seminarId, vehicleModelNumber, seminarDate, seatsBooked, status, createdAt, updatedAt
Post-Condition: Registration history retrieved successfully

Test Case: Verify authenticated user can view specific registration details
Pre-Conditions: User logged in and owns registration with ID
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /api/registrations/{id} with Authorization header
2. Verify response status code
3. Check registration details are returned
Test Data:
Authorization Header: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Path Parameter: id=<registration_id>
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 200
Response contains: {"id": <reg_id>, "seminarId": <sem_id>, "vehicleModelNumber": "<model>", "seminarDate": "<date>", "seatsBooked": <seats>, "status": "<status>", "createdAt": "<timestamp>", "updatedAt": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Registration details retrieved successfully

Test Case: Verify authenticated user can cancel their registration
Pre-Conditions: User logged in and has active registration for future seminar
Test Steps:
1. Send PUT request to /api/registrations/{id}/cancel with Authorization header
2. Verify response status code
3. Check registration status changed to CANCEL
Test Data:
Authorization Header: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Path Parameter: id=<registration_id>
Post-Condition: Registration status changed to CANCEL, seats released back to seminar

Test Case: Verify system rejects login with wrong password
Pre-Conditions: User registered and verified
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/auth/login with incorrect password
2. Verify response status code
3. Check error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 401
Response contains: {"status": 401, "message": "Invalid email or password", "errors": ["Invalid email or password"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Login rejected, no token issued

Test Case: Verify system rejects profile access without token
Pre-Conditions: No authentication token provided
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /api/users/profile without Authorization header
2. Verify response status code
3. Check error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 401
Response contains: {"status": 401, "message": "Unauthorized access", "errors": ["Unauthorized access"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Access denied

Test Case: Verify system validates required fields during registration
Pre-Conditions: System is running
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/auth/register with missing email field
2. Verify response status code
3. Check validation error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 400
Response contains: {"status": 400, "message": "Validation failed", "errors": ["email: Email is required"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Registration rejected due to validation error

Test Case: Verify system validates email format
Pre-Conditions: System is running
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/auth/register with invalid email format
2. Verify response status code
3. Check validation error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 400
Response contains: {"status": 400, "message": "Validation failed", "errors": ["email: Invalid email format"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Registration rejected due to invalid email format

Test Case: Verify system validates password length
Pre-Conditions: System is running
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/auth/register with password less than 6 characters
2. Verify response status code
3. Check validation error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 400
Response contains: {"status": 400, "message": "Validation failed", "errors": ["password: Password must be at least 6 characters"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Registration rejected due to short password

Test Case: Verify system validates seats booked range
Pre-Conditions: User logged in, seminar exists
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register with seatsBooked=3
2. Verify response status code
3. Check validation error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 400
Response contains: {"status": 400, "message": "Validation failed", "errors": ["seatsBooked: Maximum 2 seats per registration"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Registration rejected due to invalid seats count

Test Case: Verify system handles non-existent vehicle ID
Pre-Conditions: System is running
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /api/vehicles/99999
2. Verify response status code
3. Check error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 404
Response contains: {"status": 404, "message": "Vehicle not found with id: 99999", "errors": ["Vehicle not found with id: 99999"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: 404 error returned for non-existent vehicle

Test Case: Verify system handles non-existent seminar ID
Pre-Conditions: User logged in
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/seminars/99999/register with Authorization header
2. Verify response status code
3. Check error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 404
Response contains: {"status": 404, "message": "Seminar not found with id: 99999", "errors": ["Seminar not found with id: 99999"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: 404 error returned for non-existent seminar

Test Case: Verify system rejects expired OTP codes
Pre-Conditions: User registered, OTP expired (>10 minutes old)
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/auth/verify-email with expired OTP
2. Verify response status code
3. Check error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 400
Response contains: {"status": 400, "message": "Verification code has expired. Please register again.", "errors": ["Verification code has expired. Please register again."], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Verification rejected due to expired OTP

Test Case: Verify system prevents login for unverified users
Pre-Conditions: User registered but email not verified
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/auth/login with valid credentials
2. Verify response status code
3. Check error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 401
Response contains: {"status": 401, "message": "Please verify your email before logging in", "errors": ["Please verify your email before logging in"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Login rejected for unverified user

Test Case: Verify system prevents duplicate email registration
Pre-Conditions: User already registered and verified with email
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/auth/register with existing verified email
2. Verify response status code
3. Check error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 400
Response contains: {"status": 400, "message": "Email already registered and verified. Please login.", "errors": ["Email already registered and verified. Please login."], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Registration rejected due to duplicate email

Test Case: Verify user is waitlisted when seminar is full
Pre-Conditions: User logged in, seminar has no available seats
Test Steps:
1. Send POST request to /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register with Authorization header
2. Verify response status code
3. Check registration status is WAIT
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 200
Response contains: {"id": <reg_id>, "seminarId": <sem_id>, "vehicleModelNumber": "<model>", "seminarDate": "<date>", "seatsBooked": 1, "status": "WAIT", "createdAt": "<timestamp>", "updatedAt": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Registration created with WAIT status, no seats deducted

Test Case: Verify system prevents cancelling already cancelled registration
Pre-Conditions: User logged in, has registration with CANCEL status
Test Steps:
1. Send PUT request to /api/registrations/{id}/cancel with Authorization header
2. Verify response status code
3. Check error message
Test Data:
Authorization Header: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Path Parameter: id=<cancelled_registration_id>
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 400
Response contains: {"status": 400, "message": "Registration is already cancelled", "errors": ["Registration is already cancelled"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Cancellation rejected for already cancelled registration

Test Case: Verify system rejects expired JWT tokens
Pre-Conditions: User has JWT token that expired (>24 hours old)
Test Steps:
1. Send GET request to /api/users/profile with expired JWT token
2. Verify response status code
3. Check error message
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 401
Response contains: {"status": 401, "message": "Invalid or expired token", "errors": ["Invalid or expired token"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Access denied due to expired token
